messages.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete?

mfa.title: 2FA Management
mfa.reset_confirm_msg: Are you sure you want to reset 2FA for

api_keys_page.description: Manage API keys for domains in this company
api_keys_page.info_message: Select a domain to view and manage its API keys
api_keys_page.key_created_success: API key created. Copy the secret now — it will not be shown again.
api_keys_page.key_name_desc: A descriptive name for this API key
api_keys_page.key_rotated: API key rotated. Copy the new secret.
api_keys_page.name: Name
api_keys_page.no_domains: No domains found for this company
api_keys_page.no_keys: No API keys yet. Create one to get started.
api_keys_page.rotate_warning: The old secret is now invalid. Copy and store the new secret securely.
api_keys_page.secret_desc: Copy this secret now. It will not be shown again.

auth_policy_page.title: Password & Auth Policy
auth_policy_page.description: Configure password rules, MFA requirements, and session settings

rate_limit_page.title: Send Rate Limit Policy
rate_limit_page.description: Control outbound email send rates per company and per user
rate_limit_page.per_user_hour: Max Emails per Hour per User (0 = unlimited)
rate_limit_page.per_user_day: Max Emails per Day per User (0 = unlimited)

session_page.title: Session Management
session_page.description: Configure session timeouts and view active sessions

retention_page.title: Data Retention Policy
retention_page.description: Configure how long data is retained before automatic deletion
retention_page.auto_purge_desc: Automatically delete data older than the configured retention period
retention_page.warning: Warning: Enabling auto-purge will permanently delete data beyond the retention period. This action cannot be undone.
retention_page.scope_note: Company-level policy applies to all domains unless overridden.

ip_access_page.title: IP Access Control
ip_access_page.description: Control access by IP address per protocol
ip_access_page.allowlist_desc: Only these IPs/CIDRs can connect. Empty = allow all.

domain_settings_page.description: Configure per-domain security, password policy, quota, and user registration settings.
domain_settings_page.mode_invite_hint: Users receive an invite link and set their own password. Recommended when email is configured.
domain_settings_page.mode_temp_hint: Admin sets a temporary password. User must change it on first login. Use when email is not yet configured.
domain_settings_page.registration_mode_desc: How new users are onboarded to this domain.
domain_settings_page.title: Domain Settings

users_page.invite_created: User created. Share the invite link with the user to let them set their password.
users_page.invite_link_desc: This link expires in 72 hours. Send it to the user.
users_page.mode_email_invite_info: Email invite mode: user will set their own password via the invite link.
users_page.mode_temp_password_info: Temp password mode: set a temporary password. User must change it on first login.
users_page.password_required: Password is required in temporary password mode
users_page.temp_password_desc: User will be required to change this password on first login.
users_page.title: Users

users_bulk.format_hint: CSV format: email,display_name,domain_id,password (one per row, no header)
users_bulk.drop_or_click: Drop CSV file or click to upload

onboarding.step1_info: Provide basic information about the new tenant company. This will create a company record that domains and users are attached to.
onboarding.step2_info: A valid domain is required to receive and send email. You will verify DNS ownership in the next step.
onboarding.step3_info: Add the following DNS records to your domain registrar. DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours. You can skip and verify later.
onboarding.step4_info: DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) adds a digital signature to outgoing email to help prevent spoofing. You can skip this step and configure DKIM later.
onboarding.step5_info: Create an initial mailbox user for this tenant. You can skip this and add users later from the Users page.
onboarding.review_info: Review the configuration below and click Complete Setup to finish provisioning the tenant.
onboarding.quota_gb_constraint: Total storage quota for this company in gigabytes
onboarding.domain_name_constraint: e.g. example.com — must be a valid domain
onboarding.display_name_constraint: Human-readable name shown in the UI (optional)
onboarding.dkim_selector_constraint: Typically "default" — used in the DNS record name
onboarding.private_key_pem_constraint: RSA private key in PEM format
onboarding.public_key_dns_constraint: The value to publish in the DKIM TXT record
onboarding.user_email_constraint: Must be within the domain configured in Step 2
onboarding.user_password_constraint: User will be prompted to change on first login
onboarding.error_name_required: Company name must be at least 2 characters
onboarding.error_quota_invalid: Enter a valid number greater than 0
onboarding.error_public_key_required: Public key DNS value is required

dmarc_page.title: DMARC / SPF Policy
dmarc_page.description: Configure email authentication policies to improve deliverability and protect your domain
dmarc_page.dmarc_desc: Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance
dmarc_page.dmarc_policy_hint: Start with 'none' to monitor, then tighten to 'quarantine' or 'reject'
dmarc_page.rua_hint: Receive aggregate reports about DMARC failures
dmarc_page.ruf_hint: Receive detailed reports about individual DMARC failures
dmarc_page.reject_warning: Reject policy will block emails that fail DMARC. Ensure SPF and DKIM are fully configured before enabling.
dmarc_page.spf_desc: Sender Policy Framework — specifies which servers may send email for your domain
dmarc_page.spf_info: SPF records are published as TXT records on your domain. Changes here generate the record value you must publish in DNS.
dmarc_page.spf_includes_desc: Other domains authorized to send email on your behalf
dmarc_page.spf_ip4_desc: CIDR ranges or individual IPs allowed to send email
dmarc_page.records_desc: Publish these records in your DNS provider to activate the policy

spam_filter_page.title: Spam & Content Filter
spam_filter_page.description: Configure spam detection thresholds, virus scanning, and sender policies
spam_filter_page.enabled_desc: Enable or disable spam and content filtering for this company
spam_filter_page.virus_scan_desc: Scan all incoming attachments for known malware signatures
spam_filter_page.action_desc: What to do when a message exceeds the spam threshold
spam_filter_page.max_attachment_hint: 0 = unlimited. Attachments exceeding this size will be rejected.
spam_filter_page.blocked_ext_desc: Messages containing attachments with these extensions will be blocked
spam_filter_page.blocked_senders_desc: Email addresses or domain patterns (@domain.com) always blocked
spam_filter_page.allowed_senders_desc: These senders bypass all spam checks
spam_filter_page.allowed_senders_warning: Allowed senders bypass all spam filtering — use with caution
spam_filter_page.save_error: Failed to save spam filter policy

quota_dashboard_page.title: Quota Dashboard
quota_dashboard_page.description: Overview of storage quota usage across all users and domains
quota_dashboard_page.over_limit_warning: Some users or domains have exceeded their quota limit
quota_dashboard_page.name: Name

routing_rules_page.title: Routing Rules
routing_rules_page.description: Define rules to forward, BCC, reject, or tag incoming and outgoing mail
routing_rules_page.empty_state: No routing rules configured. Add a rule to control mail flow.
routing_rules_page.field_match_from_hint: Glob pattern or empty to match all (e.g. *@spam.com)
routing_rules_page.field_match_to_hint: Glob pattern or empty to match all
routing_rules_page.field_match_subject_hint: Substring match or empty to match all
routing_rules_page.field_action_value_hint: Forward/BCC: email address; Reject: message; Tag: tag name
routing_rules_page.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this rule?

sso_page.title: SSO / SAML Configuration
sso_page.description: Configure single sign-on for your organization
sso_page.status_enabled: SSO is enabled. Users can sign in using your identity provider.
sso_page.status_disabled: SSO is not enabled. Enable it below to allow identity provider sign-in.
sso_page.enabled_desc: Allow users to sign in via your identity provider
sso_page.force_sso_desc: Require all users to authenticate via SSO
sso_page.force_sso_warning: Users won't be able to log in with passwords when Force SSO is enabled
sso_page.auto_provision_desc: Automatically create user accounts on first SSO login
sso_page.entity_id_hint: Register this Entity ID with your identity provider
sso_page.metadata_url_desc: URL of your identity provider's SAML metadata document
sso_page.sso_login_url_desc: Fallback IdP SSO endpoint if metadata URL is not available
sso_page.certificate_desc: Public certificate from your identity provider for signature verification
sso_page.attribute_email_hint: SAML attribute name containing the user's email address
sso_page.attribute_name_hint: SAML attribute name containing the user's display name

smtp_policy_page.title: Outbound SMTP Policy
smtp_policy_page.description: Configure outbound mail delivery settings for this domain
smtp_policy_page.select_domain: Select a domain to view its SMTP policy
smtp_policy_page.tls_required_desc: Enforce TLS for all outbound SMTP connections
smtp_policy_page.tls_required_warning: Requiring TLS may cause delivery failures to servers that don't support it
smtp_policy_page.starttls_desc: Advertise and use STARTTLS when available
smtp_policy_page.retry_count_hint: Number of delivery attempts before giving up (1–10)
smtp_policy_page.connection_timeout_hint: Seconds to wait for SMTP connection
smtp_policy_page.helo_hostname_hint: Leave empty to use system default
smtp_policy_page.dedicated_ip_info: Contact support to request dedicated outbound IPs
